routing_policy.standing_rule: Evaluate implicit skill triggers on every non-trivial task before editing files or running long commands.
routing_policy.selection_rule: Use the smallest skill set that changes the approach, then read each selected SKILL.md before task work.
routing_policy.conflict_rule: Project instructions and HANDOFF.md override generic skill guidance for this workspace.

project_signals: local LoRA QLoRA SFT training runs
training run receipts and VRAM estimates

scenarios.use_when: Use when adding or changing data that teaches a model how to operate inside Codex.
project_guardrails: Do not edit EveryDream trainer internals for app-environment data.

scenarios.use_when: Use when changing trainer launch paths, run planning, local model/dataset discovery, VRAM estimates, or training artifacts.
project_guardrails: Do not load models or start real training unless Shawn explicitly asks.
Treat Project MoK datasets as external inputs, not workspace ownership.
Keep training state, runs, models, and weights out of git.

scenarios.use_when: Use when the local skill map is missing, stale, or directly requested.
Use for targeted dashboard rendering, layout, or browser behavior changes.
project_guardrails: Keep the first screen focused on training metrics.
Keep full TensorBoard behind the in-app popup.

scenarios.use_when: Use normal coding workflow for backend route and service changes unless another skill is explicitly triggered.
Consider for larger audits of corpus quality, coverage, or holdout separation.
outputs: validation report or dataset quality notes

scenarios.use_when: Use when current OpenAI product or API behavior is needed.
project_guardrails: Use official OpenAI sources for current product details.
